Operation 'createPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks critical details about inputs (what CreatePaymentRequest contains), outputs (what PaymentResponse returns, specifically pspReferenceId), and side effects (payment order created in pending status). AI agents need this information to determine if this operation matches their intent and how to use the response.

data.current_description: Create a new payment order for checkout processing.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new payment order for checkout processing. Accepts payment details including amount, currency, and customer information via CreatePaymentRequest. Returns a PaymentResponse containing the payment order ID (pspReferenceId), status, and checkout details. The payment order is initialized in pending status and ready for customer authorization.
data.reasoning: Description lacks critical details about inputs (what CreatePaymentRequest contains), outputs (what PaymentResponse returns, specifically pspReferenceId), and side effects (payment order created in pending status). AI agents need this information to determine if this operation matches their intent and how to use the response.

message: Operation 'getPaymentStatus' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing clarity on required input (pspReferenceId parameter) and what specific information is returned in the response. AI agents need to understand the parameter requirement and response structure to use this operation effectively.

data.current_description: Retrieve the current status of a payment order.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status of a payment order using its pspReferenceId. Returns a PaymentStatusResponse containing the payment status (e.g., pending, completed, failed, cancelled) and relevant transaction details. Use this operation to poll payment progress or verify payment completion.
data.reasoning: Missing clarity on required input (pspReferenceId parameter) and what specific information is returned in the response. AI agents need to understand the parameter requirement and response structure to use this operation effectively.

message: Operation 'cancelPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks input parameter details (pspReferenceId), output information (what PaymentResponse contains), and important side effects (status transition, irreversibility). AI agents need these details to understand preconditions and consequences of cancellation.

data.current_description: Cancel a payment order that is in pending status.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels a payment order in pending status using its pspReferenceId. Only payments in pending status can be cancelled. Returns a PaymentResponse confirming the cancellation with updated status. Side effect: The payment order status transitions to cancelled and cannot be reused.
data.reasoning: Description lacks input parameter details (pspReferenceId), output information (what PaymentResponse contains), and important side effects (status transition, irreversibility). AI agents need these details to understand preconditions and consequences of cancellation.

message: Operation 'createRefund' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Missing details about input structure (RefundRequest parameters), output structure (RefundResponse fields including refundId), and important side effects (asynchronous processing, payment amount reduction). AI agents need this information to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Request a full or partial refund for a completed payment.
data.description_suggestion: Requests a full or partial refund for a completed payment using its pspReferenceId. Accepts RefundRequest containing refund amount and reason. Returns a RefundResponse with refund ID (refundId), status, and amount. Side effect: Refund request is created in pending status and will be processed asynchronously. Original payment amount is reduced by refund amount upon completion.
data.reasoning: Missing details about input structure (RefundRequest parameters), output structure (RefundResponse fields including refundId), and important side effects (asynchronous processing, payment amount reduction). AI agents need this information to properly construct requests and handle responses.

data.current_description: Retrieve the current status of a refund request.

data.current_description: Create a new final beneficiary/recipient for payments.

data.current_description: Generate an authorization URL for OAuth2 flow.

data.description_suggestion: Generates an OAuth2 authorization URL that clients redirect users to for authentication and consent. Accepts client credentials and redirect parameters. message: Operation 'initiateBackchannelAuth' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about inputs, outputs, and the workflow. Added clarity on what information is required, what is returned (request ID and confirmation), and how this operation relates to the next step (OTP validation). This enables AI agents to understand the multi-step authentication process.

data.current_description: Initiate Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication flow with OTP.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate backchannel authentication with OTP delivery
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a Client-Initiated Backchannel Authentication (CIBA) flow by sending an OTP to the user's registered contact method. Accepts user identifier and authentication context. Returns an authentication request ID and OTP delivery confirmation. The user must validate the OTP using the completeBackchannelAuth operation to obtain authorization.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs, outputs, and the workflow. Added clarity on what information is required, what is returned (request ID and confirmation), and how this operation relates to the next step (OTP validation). This enables AI agents to understand the multi-step authentication process.

message: Operation 'completeBackchannelAuth' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary was too narrow (only mentioned validation, not completion). Description lacked input requirements and output details. Enhanced to clarify the relationship to the previous operation, what inputs are needed, what is returned, and potential side effects (rate limiting, lockout). This helps AI agents understand the complete authentication flow.

data.current_description: Complete backchannel authentication by validating the OTP code.
data.summary_suggestion: Validate OTP and complete backchannel authentication
data.description_suggestion: Completes the backchannel authentication flow by validating the OTP code provided by the user. Requires the authentication request ID from initiateBackchannelAuth and the OTP code. Returns an authorization token or code upon successful validation. Failed validation attempts may trigger rate limiting or account lockout protections.
data.reasoning: Summary was too narrow (only mentioned validation, not completion). Description lacked input requirements and output details. Enhanced to clarify the relationship to the previous operation, what inputs are needed, what is returned, and potential side effects (rate limiting, lockout). This helps AI agents understand the complete authentication flow.

message: Operation 'resendOtp' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description is vague about when and why this operation is used. Added context about the authentication request ID requirement, what is returned, and typical use cases (non-receipt or expiration). This helps AI agents determine when to invoke this operation within the authentication workflow.

data.current_description: Resend the OTP code for backchannel authentication.
data.summary_suggestion: Resend OTP to user contact method
data.description_suggestion: Resends the OTP code to the user's registered contact method during an active backchannel authentication session. Requires the authentication request ID from the initial initiateBackchannelAuth call. Returns confirmation of OTP delivery. Typically used when the user did not receive the initial OTP or the code has expired.
data.reasoning: Original description is vague about when and why this operation is used. Added context about the authentication request ID requirement, what is returned, and typical use cases (non-receipt or expiration). This helps AI agents determine when to invoke this operation within the authentication workflow.

Summary is adequate but description lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. data.current_description: Exchange authorization code or refresh token for access token.
data.summary_suggestion: Exchange authorization code or refresh token for access token
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges an authorization code or refresh token for an access token to authenticate API requests. Accepts either an authorization code (from OAuth2 flow) or a refresh token (for token renewal). Returns an access token, token type, expiration time, and optionally a new refresh token. Used to obtain credentials for subsequent API calls.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. Added clarity on the two exchange scenarios (authorization code vs refresh token), what parameters are returned (token type, expiration, optional refresh token), and the purpose of the returned token. This helps AI agents understand both initial token acquisition and token refresh scenarios.
